sections.paragraph:
roots also unused [omitted in Ges. corr.], which appear to have had the sense of flowing together; transferred from water to men, whence גֹּוי people, prop. confluence of men [but see above]; גַּיְא, גַּי a valley so called from the confluence of water there. Kindred are the Arab. جوى seq. ب and Conj. IV. to gather camels together to the water, جِوَأُ valley, level country, also جَاءَ to come, جَأْجَأَ to gather camels together to the water, جِيءَةُ, جِيْىءَةُ, contr. جِيَّةُ a place where water flows together, a valley, a low region.

I. גֵּוָה f., i.q. גֵּו body, Job 20:25. Root גָּוָה No. I.

II. גֵּוָה f. contr. for גֵּאֲוָה (from the root גַּאָה).-

(1) lifting up, exaltation; Job 22:29, וַתֹּאמֶר גֵּוָה כִּי הִשְׁפִּילוּ “when (men) act humbly, thou commandest lifting up,” i.e. thou liftest up the modest, meek men. Commonly rendered, “when thy ways are humbled (verse 28 Job 22:28), thou shalt say, lifting up,” i.e. thou presently perceivest thy state, from the lowest to become most prosperous.

(2) pride, arrogance, Jer 13:17 Job 33:17.
